verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It is synthesized with a signal peptide, processed through the secretory pathway, and released into extracellular fluid (seminal plasma, serum) as a soluble enzyme — it has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no outer-leaflet lipidation. Pre-checklist: (1) No cell-surface-directed antibody/ADC/CAR-T program targets KLK2 on the cell surface; diagnostic/prognostic use is serum-based. (2) Not a TM precursor with ectodomain shedding — it is constitutively secreted. (3) No documented wash-resistant TM-partner tethering. (4) No documented minority PM pool. (5) No cancer ecto-display, cell-death surfacing, germline-restricted surface display, or activation-induced PM translocation documented. The soluble secreted pool is the canonical biological form; it is not in equilibrium with a stable cell-surface anchor. Verdict: secreted_only.
